The Frontiers of HCI Research

HCI-related seminars and talks at Johns Hopkins University

Upcoming Talks & Events

September 27, 2019 – Prof. Marynel Vázquez – Yale University

Marynel will have a fireside chat with students about human-centered robotics in the Intuitive Computing Laboratory.

Marynel Vázquez is a computer scientist and roboticist. Her research focuses on understanding how to build interactive computing technologies that are meaningful, intuitive, and appropriate for users, especially in complex social environments. In particular, her research agenda centers on enabling social interactions between robots and multiple people. Marynel’s research crosses the boundaries between Human-Robot and Human-Computer Interaction, and often combines elements from robot perception, computer vision, machine learning, social science, and design.

October 3, 2019 – Prof. Zhou Yu – UC Davis

Humans interact with other humans or the world through information from various channels including vision, audio, language, haptics, etc. To simulate intelligence, machines require similar abilities to process and combine information from different channels to acquire better situation awareness, better communication ability, and better decision-making ability. In this talk, we describe three projects covering multimodal user understanding, visual dialog optimization, and social multimodal dialog generation.

Zhou Yu is an Assistant Professor at the Computer Science Department at UC Davis. She received her PhD from Carnegie Mellon University in 2017. Zhou is interested in building robust and multi-purpose dialog systems. Her work on persuasive dialog systems received an ACL 2019 best paper nomination. She was featured in Forbes as 2018 30 Under 30 in Science, and her team won the 2018 Amazon Alexa Prize for a $500,000 cash award.

November 7, 2019 – Prof. Jodi Forlizzi – Carnegie Mellon University

A recent New York Times article boldly stated that the Golden Age of Design is upon us. This talk will reflect a walk through the discipline of design’s many eras and shifts — from user-centered design to experience design, service design, and systems design — to understand the migration from designing products to designing platforms. An alternative framing, product-service ecologies, will be introduced to stress a systemic and ecological view as a design approach to the products, services, environments, and platforms of today.

Jodi Forlizzi is the Geschke Director and a Professor of Human-Computer Interaction in the School of Computer Science at Carnegie Mellon University. She is responsible for establishing design research as a legitimate form of research in HCI. Jodi is a member of the ACM CHI Academy and has been honored by the Walter Reed Army Medical Center for excellence in HRI design research. She has consulted with Disney and General Motors to create innovative product-service systems.
